title: Verilog compile hangs with XMR to function inside genblk
body:
Failing test here -
https://github.com/VarunKoyyalagunta/verilator/commit/ce04c291bb093e35f7d181cf70db3ca0984bd0e2
```
$ ./test_regress/t/t_xmr_genblk_function.pl --debug --debugi-V3LinkDot 9
- V3LinkDot.cpp:673:      findSymPrefixed block__DOT__set under se0x5555562df000 as t__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set  at se0x5555562df000
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562df000 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562ded80 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562dece0 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562df000 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562ded80 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562dece0 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562df000 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562ded80 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562dece0 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
- V3SymTable.h:151:        SymFind   se0x5555562df000 't__DOT__test__DOT__subtest__DOT__block__DOT__block__DOT__set' -> NONE
[last message repeated forever]
```

verilator --version:
Tried `4.220` and master (`Verilator 4.221 devel rev v4.220-26-g880a9be3`)

OS:
Linux

I can work around this by creating an additional layer of module hierarchy inside the genblk that encapsulates the function.

I don't really have a lot of time to help out with this right now, but thought I'd report it and can look into fixing it later if it's still open.
